output.final_deliverable:
Results

1) Exact H1 text from https://example.com

- Extraction: Example Domain
- Verbatim quote from the page (RAW VISIT CONTENT): "Example Domain"
- Source / citation: https://example.com

2) Exact domain of the external link from https://example.com

- Extraction (exact domain): iana.org
- Verbatim evidence that the page contains link text: "Learn more" (from the example.com RAW VISIT CONTENT). Quote: "Learn more" (https://example.com)
- Verbatim evidence showing the external link URL found by the visit/scan: "https://iana.org/domains/example" (from the visit record for https://example.com)
  - Quote from the visit record: "links": ["https://iana.org/domains/example"] (source: visit result for https://example.com)
- Source / citation for the link target: https://iana.org/domains/example

3) One URL citation for each visited page

- https://example.com (page visited; H1 and page text quoted above)
- https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved (IANA Reserved Domains page visited)

Supporting verbatim quotes from the RAW VISIT CONTENT (primary evidence)

- From https://example.com (RAW VISIT CONTENT):
  "Example Domain
  Example Domain
  This domain is for use in documentation examples without needing permission. Avoid use in operations.
  Learn more"
  (https://example.com)

- From https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved (RAW VISIT CONTENT):
  "IANA-managed Reserved Domains"
  (https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved)

- Also from the IANA page (showing example domains mention) (RAW VISIT CONTENT):
  "As described in
  RFC 2606
  and
  RFC 6761
  ,
   a number of domains such as
  example.com
  and
  example.org
  are maintained for documentation purposes. These domains may be used as illustrative
   examples in documents without prior coordination with us. They are 
   not available for registration or transfer."
  (https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved)

Notes on evidence used

- The H1 text "Example Domain" was taken directly from the example.com RAW VISIT CONTENT (see quote above) and is cited to https://example.com.
- The presence of a link labeled "Learn more" is shown in the example.com RAW VISIT CONTENT (see quote above). The visit record for https://example.com lists the external link URL exactly as "https://iana.org/domains/example", from which the link's domain is iana.org. Both the example.com visit record and the link target URL are cited above.
- The IANA reserved page visit and its title / supporting text are quoted directly from the RAW VISIT CONTENT for https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved and are cited to that URL.

All factual claims above are supported by the quoted raw page content and the visit record link list. URL citations for every claim are provided inline above.
output.action_summary: Returned: (1) H1 from https://example.com is exactly "Example Domain" (quoted from the page); (2) the external link on example.com points to https://iana.org/domains/example, so the exact domain is "iana.org" (visit record: "https://iana.org/domains/example" and the example.com page shows the link text "Learn more"); (3) URL citations: https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved (both quoted). All quotations and citations provided.

result.content:
IANA-managed Reserved Domains
Certain domains are set aside, and nominally registered to “IANA”, for specific
		policy or technical purposes.
Example domains
As described in
RFC 2606
and
RFC 6761
,
	a number of domains such as
example.com
and
example.org
are maintained for documentation purposes. These domains may be used as illustrative
	examples in documents without prior coordination with us. They are 
	not available for registration or transfer.
Test IDN top-level domains
These domains were temporarily delegated by IANA for the
IDN Evaluation
being conducted by
ICANN
.

Policy-reserved domains
We act as both the registrant and registrar for a select number of domains
		which have been reserved under policy grounds. These exclusions are
		typically indicated in either technical standards (RFC documents),
		or
contractual limitations
.
Domains which are described as registered to IANA or ICANN on policy
		grounds are not available for registration or transfer, with the exception
		of
country-name
.info
domains. These domains are available for release
		by the ICANN Governmental Advisory Committee Secretariat.
Other Special-Use Domains
There is additionally a
Special-Use Domain Names
registry documenting special-use domains designated by technical standards. For further information, see
Special-Use Domain Names
(RFC 6761).

f8762d7f-bda1-4877-b11c-628b0d0f9cec.title: Visit https://example.com and extract H1, external link domain, and citation

details.extraction_intent: Fetch the page HTML and extract (1) the exact text content of the primary H1 element on the page, (2) the exact domain (hostname only, e.g. example.org) of the external link found on the page (the 'Learn more' link), and (3) a single URL citation for this visited page (the exact URL visited).

output_fields.name: h1_text
output_fields.description: The exact text content of the first/top-level <h1> element on https://example.com

output_fields.name: external_link_domain
output_fields.description: The exact domain/hostname of the external link on the page (no scheme, no path), e.g. 'iana.org'

output_fields.name: page_citation_url
output_fields.description: A single URL citation for the visited page (the exact URL visited)

details.why: Directly visiting the exact URL given ensures we capture the page's live H1 text and the actual external link, eliminating uncertainty from search snippets or cached summaries.
details.goal: Visit https://example.com and extract H1, external link domain, and citation
details.original_goal: Visit https://example.com and extract H1, external link domain, and citation
details.parent_goal: Visit https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved. Return: (1) the exact H1 text from example.com, (2) the exact domain of the external link from example.com, and (3) one URL citation for each visited page.

c6662e22-84c9-441b-966a-0e744f08e5c8.title: Visit https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved and produce one URL citation

details.extraction_intent: Fetch the page and return one URL citation for this visited page (the exact URL visited). Optionally capture the page title if available for verification.

output_fields.name: page_citation_url
output_fields.description: A single URL citation for the visited page (the exact URL visited)

output_fields.name: page_title
output_fields.description: Optional: the visible page title or H1 for verification (if present)

details.why: User explicitly requested a citation for this IANA page; visiting the exact URL ensures the returned citation is authoritative and exact.
details.goal: Visit https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved and produce one URL citation
details.original_goal: Visit https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved and produce one URL citation
details.parent_goal: Visit https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved. Return: (1) the exact H1 text from example.com, (2) the exact domain of the external link from example.com, and (3) one URL citation for each visited page.

Policy-reserved domains
We act as both the registrant and registrar for a select number of domains
		which have been reserved under policy grounds. These exclusions are
		typically indicated in either technical standards (RFC documents),
		or
contractual limitations
.
Domains which are described as registered to IANA or ICANN on policy
		grounds are not available for registration or transfer, with the exception
		of
country-name
.info
domains. These domains are available for release
		by the ICANN Governmental Advisory Committee Secretariat.
Other Special-Use Domains
There is additionally a
Special-Use Domain Names
registry documenting special-use domains designated by technical standards. For further information, see
Special-Use Domain Names
(RFC 6761).
result.content_full:
IANA-managed Reserved Domains
Certain domains are set aside, and nominally registered to “IANA”, for specific
		policy or technical purposes.
Example domains
As described in
RFC 2606
and
RFC 6761
,
	a number of domains such as
example.com
and
example.org
are maintained for documentation purposes. These domains may be used as illustrative
	examples in documents without prior coordination with us. They are 
	not available for registration or transfer.
Test IDN top-level domains
These domains were temporarily delegated by IANA for the
IDN Evaluation
being conducted by
ICANN
.

details.goal_evaluation: All requested outputs were produced and can be verified in the child node results: (1) the exact H1 from example.com is provided and matches the h1_text field; (2) the external link on example.com is present in links_full and its domain was extracted as iana.org; (3) each visited page has an explicit URL citation in its source_url field. The objective (not just keyword presence but the actual required values) is satisfied.
